headline: 467 NASAO airframes grounded across 25 states with $50M–$2B national exposure as gray zone hardware teardowns confirm adversary control

lead_story.title: NASAO Grounding Event: 467 Airframes Across 23 States
lead_story.body: Oregon's NASAO Revision 2 white paper (February 2026) documents 467 drone airframes grounded or restricted across 23 states based on a survey of 25 state DOTs, creating national exposure between $50M and $2B. This represents the largest documented compliance event in the Blue UAS transition to date. The grounding coincides with hardware teardowns confirming that multiple gray zone manufacturers—Anzu Robotics, Cogito/SPECTA, and SkyRover—are shipping relabeled adversary hardware with adversary-controlled cryptographic keys and cloud infrastructure. State DOTs are frozen mid-procurement with no clear replacement pathway.
lead_story.action: Audit all airframes purchased between 2023–2025 against the FCC Covered List (updated Dec 22, 2025) and NDAA Section 1709 manufacturer exclusions; identify NASAO-affected inventory and initiate replacement planning with 90-day lead time assumption.

gray_zone.status: Subject to FCC Covered List; TX DTPA lawsuit filed; direct DJI cloud connectivity confirmed in teardown
gray_zone.development: Independent researcher Jon Sawyer confirmed SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure using identical encryption keys. OTA update pipeline routes through adversary infrastructure. Shell company filings trace to SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) inventory remains available at retail despite FCC prohibition on new authorizations.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: State and local agencies using SkyRover S1/X1 face direct data exfiltration risk to adversary cloud; firmware updates are adversary-controlled; no airgap possible
gray_zone.action: Ground all SkyRover units immediately; quarantine mission data; initiate forensic review of all flight logs and imagery uploaded since first deployment; do not process firmware updates

gray_zone.status: TX DTPA lawsuit filed Feb 18, 2026 (7 counts); no response filed after 45+ days; PCB-level adversary hardware confirmed
gray_zone.development: Teardown confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise with matching board part numbers. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI hardware. Firmware is signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys—DJI retains root key control. Product line discontinued due to adversary component shortage. Texas seeks injunctive relief, civil penalties up to $10K/violation, and compliance orders.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Agencies using Anzu Raptor series face firmware backdoor risk, adversary supply chain dependency, and potential civil enforcement exposure under state-level deceptive trade practice statutes
gray_zone.action: Suspend Raptor operations pending legal resolution; evaluate replacement options from Blue UAS Framework; preserve evidence of purchase representations for potential recovery claims

gray_zone.status: Added to FCC Covered List Dec 22, 2025; teardown confirms identical DJI Air 3 hardware
gray_zone.development: Specta Air teardown reveals identical PCBs to DJI Air 3, same DJI P1 chipset, with RF emission reports differing only by date and logo. Technology licensed from DJI but licensing relationship was not disclosed to buyers. FCC has retroactive revocation authority as of Oct 2025 vote; no new models can receive authorization.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Public safety and infrastructure inspection operators using Specta Air face immediate grounding risk if FCC exercises retroactive revocation; no upgrade path available
gray_zone.action: Contact FCC Equipment Authorization Branch to confirm current authorization status of deployed units; develop contingency procurement plan assuming 60-day grounding notice

gray_zone.status: Named in NDAA Section 1709(a)(1); FCC Covered List as of Dec 22, 2025; certain authorizations retroactively revoked Jan 2026
gray_zone.development: FCC revoked certain pre-ban authorizations in January 2026. Autel is seeking reconsideration at FCC level, but unlike Anzu/SkyRover/Specta, Autel hardware is proprietary (not a DJI derivative). Dragonfish fixed-wing platform remains in service with some Florida and other state agencies. Section 1709 creates federal procurement bar alongside DJI.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Federal agencies face immediate NDAA compliance violation; state/local face FCC authorization uncertainty and potential future federal funding条件
gray_zone.action: Federal users must cease Autel procurement and operations immediately per NDAA; state/local users should monitor FCC reconsideration docket and prepare replacement sourcing for Q3 2026

supply_chain.signal: 87% probability of allocation tightening in H2 2026; 15 of 52 thermal camera systems in Forge DB use Boson series (29% concentration); 3 Boson units tracked in AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp
supply_chain.window: 8-10 weeks before defense allocation prioritization begins
supply_chain.action: Secure 12-month forward contracts for Boson 640 modules by end of Q2 2026; qualify secondary thermal core (TAU 2 or Neutrino) by July 2026

supply_chain.signal: 82% probability of allocation-only distribution for defense customers in Q3 2026; 16 of 25 companion computer configs use Orin NX (64% concentration); 13 boards currently deployed in Forge DB at supply risk
supply_chain.action: Request defense allocation tier status from NVIDIA by May 1; hedge with Qualcomm QRB5165 dual-source design for new programs; stockpile 6-month Orin NX inventory for production systems

supply_chain.signal: 81% probability of bottleneck in Q3-Q4 2026; 17 of 29 mesh radio options in Forge DB use concentrated vendor set (59%); only 29 total NDAA-compliant options tracked
supply_chain.action: Audit current mesh radio vendors against NDAA compliance and FCC authorization status; pre-qualify Silvus StreamCaster and Persistent Systems MPU5 as backup options; lock Q4 2026 allocation now

watch_list.item: Raspberry Pi diversion to Russian Geran/Molniya UAV programs
watch_list.why: GUR confirmed RPi 4B in 40,000+ Geran-2/3 units and RPi 5 in Molniya-2R reconnaissance drone; Geran-5 jet drone uses RPi-based tracker with mesh networking
watch_list.trigger: US/UK export control designation of RPi as dual-use technology or distributor compliance enforcement action

watch_list.why: PIE cross-correlation model shows 79% probability of simultaneous deadlines grounding uncertified gray zone drones; 4 regulatory/compliance flags matched
watch_list.trigger: FCC publishes final firmware certification requirements and grandfather clause expiration date

watch_list.item: Qualcomm QRB5165 successor entry to Blue UAS Framework
watch_list.why: 80% probability in 2027; 5 of 25 companion computer boards currently depend on QRB5165; lifecycle transition could strand existing designs
watch_list.trigger: Qualcomm announces QRB5165 EOL notice or new QRB6xxx series with Blue UAS certification application

predictions.event: FLIR Boson 640 tightens — 3 Boson units tracked, AeroV Switchblade ramp drives demand
predictions.hedge: Execute non-cancellable purchase orders for 18-month Boson 640 supply by June 30, 2026; initiate qualification testing of FLIR Hadron 640R as drop-in replacement; budget 15% premium for expedited delivery

predictions.event: Jetson Orin NX goes allocation-only for defense (13 boards in Forge DB at risk)
predictions.hedge: Register for NVIDIA Partner Network defense tier by May 15; submit 12-month forecast to NVIDIA by June 1; maintain 9-month safety stock for production programs; design-in Orin Nano as lower-performance backup

predictions.event: Mesh radio supply bottleneck — 29 options tracked, only 29 NDAA-compliant
predictions.hedge: Dual-source mesh radio procurement across Silvus and Persistent Systems by July 2026; negotiate volume pricing with 18-month delivery schedule; fund internal integration testing for 3 qualified vendors by August 2026

signal_summary.top_concern: adversary-controlled cryptographic keys in deployed gray zone hardware
